# Overview [STALE] ‚Äî package: overview ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:30Z

Every LLM call in the system goes through a two-layer prompt assembly process: a **Jinja template** (the skeleton) and **prompt contributors** (dynamically injected sections). The template is selected by `AgentActionMetadata`, the contributors are filtered by agent type and retry state, and the final text is assembled by `PromptContributorService`. Understanding this assembly process is essential because the prompt is the *only* thing the LLM sees ‚Äî every behavioral difference between agents comes from which template loads and which contributors fire.

# Prompt Assembly Process [STALE] ‚Äî package: prompt-assembly-process ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:30Z

References: 9 files tracked (package: prompt-assembly-process) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Prompt Assembly Process"]` in .metadata/HEAD

When `AgentExecutor.run()` reaches step 7 (LLM call), the prompt is assembled:

```
AgentExecutor
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ Step 3: PromptContextFactory.build()
 ‚îÇ    Creates PromptContext(agentType, templateName, chatId, errorDescriptor, currentRequest)
 ‚îÇ    PromptContextDecorators modify it (DefaultPromptContextDecorator, FilterPropertiesDecorator)
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ Step 6: resolveTemplate()
 ‚îÇ    If retry with error ‚Üí swap to error-specific template from ErrorTemplates
 ‚îÇ    Otherwise ‚Üí use templateName from AgentActionMetadata
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îî‚îÄ Step 7: LlmRunner.runWithTemplate() ‚Üí PromptContributorService.assemblePrompt()
      ‚îÇ
      ‚îú‚îÄ 1. TEMPLATE RENDERING
      ‚îÇ    Embabel TemplateRenderer compiles the Jinja template
      ‚îÇ    Renders with templateModel (goal, prior results, delegation, etc.)
      ‚îÇ    ‚Üí produces the "skeleton" text
      ‚îÇ
      ‚îî‚îÄ 2. CONTRIBUTOR INJECTION
           PromptContributorRegistry.getContributors(promptContext) ‚Üí static registrations
           PromptContributorFactory.create(promptContext) ‚Üí dynamic factories
           ‚îÇ
           ‚îú‚îÄ On retry: skip factories where RetryAware.includeOnRetry(errorType) == false
           ‚îÇ
           ‚îú‚îÄ Sort by priority (lower = earlier in prompt)
           ‚îÇ
           ‚îî‚îÄ Append each contributor's output with delimiters:
                --- start [contributor-name] ---
                {content}
                --- end [contributor-name] --- start [next-name] ---
                {content}
                --- end [last-name] ---
```

The delimiter format (`--- start/end [name] ---`) is not decoration ‚Äî it lets downstream tooling and the debug UI identify which contributor produced which section.

# Jinja Template Organization [STALE] ‚Äî package: jinja-template-organization ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:30Z

References: 55 files tracked (package: jinja-template-organization) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Jinja Template Organization"]` in .metadata/HEAD

Templates map 1:1 to agent actions and are organized by concern:

```
prompts/
 ‚îú‚îÄ workflow/                    ‚Üê main agent templates
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ orchestrator.jinja       (OrchestratorRoute)
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ orchestrator_collector.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ discovery_orchestrator.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ discovery_agent.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ discovery_dispatch.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ discovery_collector.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ planning_orchestrator.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ planning_agent.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ planning_dispatch.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ planning_collector.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ ticket_orchestrator.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ ticket_agent.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ ticket_dispatch.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ ticket_collector.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ context_manager.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ context_manager_interrupt.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ review.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ review_resolution.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ merger.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ worktree_commit_agent.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ worktree_merge_conflict_agent.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ _collector_base.jinja         ‚Üê shared partial ({% include %})
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ _context_manager_body.jinja   ‚Üê shared partial
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ _interrupt_guidance.jinja     ‚Üê shared partial
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ _review_justification.jinja   ‚Üê shared partial
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ _discovery_research_disclaimer.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ _planning_disclaimer.jinja
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ justification/               ‚Üê justification templates (parallel to workflow/)
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ orchestrator.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ discovery.jinja / discovery_orchestrator.jinja / discovery_collector.jinja / discovery_dispatch.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ planning.jinja / planning_*.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ ticket.jinja / ticket_*.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ review.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ merger.jinja
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ communication/               ‚Üê inter-agent messaging
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ agent_call.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ controller_call.jinja
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ controller_response.jinja
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ filter/                      ‚Üê data-layer AI filter evaluation
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ ai_filter.jinja
 ‚îú‚îÄ propagation/                 ‚Üê data-layer AI propagator evaluation
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ ai_propagator.jinja
 ‚îú‚îÄ transformation/              ‚Üê data-layer AI transformer evaluation
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ ai_transformer.jinja
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îî‚îÄ retry/                       ‚Üê error recovery templates
      ‚îú‚îÄ compaction.jinja         (CompactionError ‚Äî first occurrence)
      ‚îú‚îÄ parse_error.jinja        (ParseError ‚Äî malformed JSON)
      ‚îú‚îÄ incomplete_json.jinja    (IncompleteJsonError)
      ‚îú‚îÄ null_result.jinja        (NullResultError ‚Äî empty LLM response)
      ‚îú‚îÄ timeout.jinja            (TimeoutError)
      ‚îî‚îÄ unparsed_tool_call.jinja (UnparsedToolCallError)
```

Partials (prefixed with `_`) are `{% include %}`d by multiple templates. The `_collector_base.jinja` is particularly important ‚Äî all three collector templates include it to get consistent consolidation instructions.

# Prompt Contributors: What Gets Injected [STALE] ‚Äî package: prompt-contributors ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:30Z

References: 24 files tracked (package: prompt-contributors) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Prompt Contributors: What Gets Injected"]` in .metadata/HEAD

Contributors add context the template doesn't have. Each runs *after* the template and is filtered by agent type:

| Contributor | What it adds | When it fires |
|---|---|---|
| **GitPromptContributorFactory** | Repository state, recent diffs, branch info | All agents with worktrees |
| **SkillPromptContributorFactory** | Skill definitions and documentation | All agents |
| **WorktreeSandboxPromptContributorFactory** | File listing from worktree path | Agents with WorktreeSandboxContext |
| **AgentTopologyPromptContributorFactory** | "You can call these agents: [list]" | Agents with topology access |
| **JustificationPromptContributorFactory** | Justification template for controller review | Agents under structured conversation |
| **JsonOutputFormatPromptContributorFactory** | JSON schema for expected output format | All agents |
| **EpisodicMemoryPromptContributor** | Past execution memories (from Hindsight) | Agents with memory enabled |
| **CurationHistoryContextContributorFactory** | Prior curation decisions from this workflow | Collectors and orchestrators |
| **WeAreHerePromptContributor** | "Current phase: DISCOVERY, position: collector" | All agents |
| **ContextManagerPromptContributorFactory** | Available return routes for context manager | Context manager only |
| **PermissionEscalationPromptContributorFactory** | Permission escalation instructions | Agents in sandboxed sessions |
| **FirstOrchestratorPromptContributorFactory** | Special first-run context for the orchestrator | First orchestrator call only |

# Retry-Aware Filtering [STALE] ‚Äî package: retry-aware-filtering ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:30Z

References: 5 files tracked (package: retry-aware-filtering) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Retry-Aware Filtering"]` in .metadata/HEAD

The retry flow spans three layers ‚Äî Embabel framework retry, error classification, template resolution, and prompt contributor filtering ‚Äî with a parallel ACP-layer retry context for transport-level decisions.

**1. Error occurs** ‚Äî The LLM call in `AgentExecutor.run()` throws (parse error, timeout, compaction, null result, incomplete JSON, unparsed tool call). The Embabel framework invokes `ActionRetryListener.onActionRetry()`.

**2. Error classification** (`ActionRetryListenerImpl.classify()`) ‚Äî Classifies the throwable into an `ErrorDescriptor` variant by inspecting the exception type and message text. Finds the current in-flight execution via `findLastUnmatchedStart()` ‚Äî walks `BlackboardHistory` backwards to find `AgentExecutorStartEvent` entries with no matching `AgentExecutorCompleteEvent` (matched by unique `nodeId`, not `requestContextId` which is shared across retries). Error counts are scoped to the current retry sequence. The classified `ErrorDescriptor` is stored on `BlackboardHistory` via `history.addError()` and published to the event bus.

**3. ACP-layer parallel tracking** ‚Äî `AcpRetryEventListener` subscribes to lifecycle events (`ChatSessionCreatedEvent`, `AgentExecutorStartEvent/CompleteEvent`, `CompactionEvent`, `NullResultEvent`, `IncompleteJsonEvent`, `UnparsedToolCallEvent`, `TimeoutEvent`) and maintains per-session `AcpSessionRetryContext` (ErrorCategory + CompactionStatus + retryCount). `AcpChatModel` queries this context to adjust transport behavior (session management, timeout tuning) independently of the template layer.

**4. Next retry: error detection** ‚Äî When `AgentExecutor.run()` fires again, step 3 reads `history.errorType(chatId)` to get the `ErrorDescriptor` for the current session. This is placed on the `DecoratorContext` and the `PromptContext`.

**5. Template resolution** (`AgentExecutor.resolveTemplate()`) ‚Äî Reads `ErrorTemplates` from `AgentActionMetadata` (the static metadata record on `AgentInterfaces.MultiAgentIdeMetadata.AgentMetadata` that maps each agent action to its templates). Pattern-matches on the `ErrorDescriptor` variant:
- `CompactionError` ‚Üí `compactionFirstTemplate` or `compactionMultipleTemplate` (two tiers)
- `ParseError` ‚Üí `parseErrorTemplate`
- `TimeoutError` ‚Üí `timeoutTemplate`
- `UnparsedToolCallError` ‚Üí `unparsedToolCallTemplate`
- `NullResultError` ‚Üí `nullResultTemplate`
- `IncompleteJsonError` ‚Üí `incompleteJsonTemplate`
Returns a `ResolvedTemplate.ErrorTemplate` with the retry template name and an `errorModel` containing `errorDetail` from the exception message. The error template replaces the normal Jinja template entirely ‚Äî the `templateModel` is swapped for the `errorModel`.

**6. Prompt contributor filtering** (`PromptContributorService.retrievePromptContributors()`) ‚Äî Checks if `promptContext.errorDescriptor()` is non-null and not `NoError`. For each `PromptContributorFactory`, calls `includeRetryAware(factory, error)` which dispatches through the `RetryAware` interface: `includeOnCompaction()`, `includeOnParseError()`, `includeOnTimeout()`, etc. Default is `false` ‚Äî contributors are excluded on retry unless they explicitly opt in. This keeps retry prompts focused on the error recovery template, not full agent context.

**The two retry layers are complementary**: Embabel retry controls *what prompt* the agent sees (template + contributor filtering). ACP retry context controls *how the transport behaves* (session management, compaction awareness). Both read from the same event stream but maintain independent state.

# AgentPretty: Controlling What the LLM Sees [STALE] ‚Äî package: agentpretty-controlling-what-the-llm-sees ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:30Z

`AgentPretty` is the serialization layer between agent results and prompt context. When a prior agent's output needs to appear in a subsequent prompt:

1. The request/result implements `AgentContext.prettyPrint()` (or `prettyPrintInterruptContinuation()`)
2. `AgentPretty` formats the output as human-readable text (not raw JSON)
3. This formatted text goes into the template model as a variable

This is important because `prettyPrint()` controls information flow between agents ‚Äî it determines what the next LLM knows about prior work. The `prettyPrintInterruptContinuation()` variant is used when the request is a continuation after an interrupt, potentially including the interrupt resolution in the printed output.

# Prompt Health Check [STALE] ‚Äî package: prompt-health-check ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:30Z

References: 1 files tracked (package: prompt-health-check) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Prompt Health Check"]` in .metadata/HEAD

`PromptHealthCheckLlmCallDecorator` (an `LlmCallDecorator`) captures the fully assembled prompt *before* it's sent to the LLM and emits it as a propagation artifact via the event bus. This enables:

- Viewing the exact prompt any agent received via the debug UI (`LlmDebugPromptsController`)
- Iterating on prompt templates without re-running the full workflow
- Diagnosing prompt quality issues by comparing assembled prompts across retries
